metadata.title: San Diego, CA — Crime & Arrest Data by Race
metadata.status: PARTIAL — agency-level race breakdowns unavailable through automated means
notes: This file compiles what could be gathered programmatically. Agency-level arrest data by race for San Diego PD (ORI CA0371100) and San Diego County Sheriff (ORI CA0370000) was NOT retrievable through any of the attempted automated sources.

The FBI CDE API (api.usa.gov/crime/fbi/cde) returns structurally valid responses but ALL data values are null for every endpoint tested — NIBRS offender/victim/offense data at agency, state, and national levels.

The FBI CDE arrest endpoints (/arrest/national/{offense}/race, /arrest/agency/{ORI}/race) require valid offense ID parameters, but the API documentation page is a JS-only SPA that cannot be scraped, and no offense IDs tested returned data.

CA DOJ OpenJustice (openjustice.doj.ca.gov) arrest datasets require user authentication (login) to download CSV/PDF files. The 'Crime in California' annual PDFs are publicly accessible but contain only STATE-LEVEL summaries.
FBI UCR data tables (ucr.fbi.gov) are behind Cloudflare anti-bot protection.
The FBI CDE webapp (cde.ucr.cjis.gov) and San Diego Open Data Portal (data.sandiego.gov) are JS-only SPAs that do not render via web_fetch.
Stanford Open Policing Project has traffic stop data (not arrest data) for some CA jurisdictions but San Diego download URLs could not be resolved.
Web search was unavailable during this session.

endpoints_tested: arrest/national/{offense}/race for 12+ offenses — all 404 or rate-limited
fbi_cde_api.result: API returns structural schema with all-null data values. Data not loaded or not available through this API.

ca_doj_openjustice.result: Public-facing SPA — data exploration works in browser but requires login for data downloads. Crime in California PDFs accessible directly (state-level only).

sandiego_open_data.result: JS-only SPA. Police datasets exist but cannot be accessed programmatically.

stanford_openpolicing.result: Traffic stop data only (not arrests). San Diego download URL pattern not found.

state_level_context.source: CA DOJ Crime in California 2023 (Table 17 — Arrests)
state_level_context.notes: State-level totals only. Agency-level (San Diego PD / SD County Sheriff) race/ethnicity breakdowns not in these PDFs. See 'next_steps' for how to obtain.

san_diego_data.reason: Agency-level arrest data by race requires manual download from CA DOJ OpenJustice or direct request to SDPD / SD County Sheriff.

estimated_placeholder.note: The following are ROUGH ESTIMATES based on San Diego's share of CA population (~3.5%) applied to state totals. NOT verified. DO NOT cite without real data.

method_1_ca_doj_openjustice.description: Create a free account at https://openjustice.doj.ca.gov/ and download the Arrests dataset.
steps: 3. Filter by: County = 'San Diego', Agency = 'San Diego PD' (or San Diego County Sheriff)
4. Export to CSV — includes race/ethnicity, age, sex, offense type
5. The downloadable 'Arrests CY 20XX' CSV files have columns: COUNTY, AGENCY, OFFENSE_LEVEL, OFFENSE_CATEGORY, RACE, ETHNICITY, GENDER, AGE_GROUP, COUNT

method_2_fbi_cde_webapp.description: Use the FBI CDE Arrest Explorer in a browser.
steps: 2. Search for 'San Diego Police Department' (ORI CA0371100) or 'San Diego County Sheriff' (ORI CA0370000)
3. Select offense categories and time range (2020-2023)
4. Export data — the webapp provides demographic breakdowns by race, ethnicity, age, sex

method_3_ca_doj_cjsc_direct.description: Request data directly from CA DOJ Criminal Justice Statistics Center.
method_3_ca_doj_cjsc_direct.notes: The CJSC can provide custom data extracts. Contact via the Public Records Act or their data request form.

method_4_sdpd_public_records.description: Submit a Public Records Act request to SDPD.
method_4_sdpd_public_records.notes: SDPD publishes annual reports and has a transparency portal. They can provide arrest statistics by race upon request.

data_schema_expected.description: When agency-level data is obtained, it should be structured as follows for Phase 2:
